tools.name: kg_build_library
tools.title: Build Entity Library
tools.description: Build a canonical entity library from web content: crawl/extract entities via TextRazor and link them to Wikidata/Wikipedia/DBpedia/Freebase. Use this to start a NEW library — for schema.org emission from an EXISTING library use kg_emit_schema/kg_emit_schema_bulk instead; to resolve one term without a full build use kg_resolve_term. Feed "pages" (pre-scraped [{url, content}], preferred — pair with a scraper for JS-heavy/blocked sites) or url/urls for a built-in plain-HTTP self-fetch fallback (no JS rendering). Capped at 500 pages (max param, default 60). Returns a runPublicId plus a kg_get poll target — the library itself is not returned inline. Metered: 1,000 credits per build, charged upfront.

pages.description: PREFERRED: pre-scraped pages ({url, content}) from a scraper (e.g. MCP Scraper extract_url/extract_site). Handles JS-rendered and blocked sites; the scraper gathers, this tool does the entity work.

url.description: Source URL for this page, for provenance in the resulting library.

content.description: Scraped page content (HTML, markdown, or plain text) to extract entities from.

format.description: Content format hint. Defaults to auto-detection when omitted.

url.description: FALLBACK: single URL to self-fetch via a built-in plain-HTTP crawler (no JS rendering). A sitemap URL is auto-crawled.

urls.description: FALLBACK: multiple URLs to self-fetch via the built-in plain-HTTP crawler (no JS rendering, capped at 500).

niche.description: Optional niche/category hint to scope entity extraction and disambiguation.

max.description: Maximum pages to include in the build, capped at 500.

whenToUse: Use to build a reusable linked-entity library from page content or URLs.
avoidWhen: Avoid for one term lookup or schema emission from an existing library.
quality.costSummary: Metered at 1,000 Thorbit credits per library build.
sideEffects: Creates a durable Knowledge Graph run and persists an unapproved library when completed.
Consumes 1,000 Thorbit credits upfront.

tools.name: kg_emit_schema
tools.title: Emit Schema.org JSON-LD
tools.description: Generate finished schema.org JSON-LD for ONE page as a single nested tree (a root Organization/LocalBusiness/Service/Article node with WebPage, services, author, and entity blocks nested beneath it — not a flat @graph of siblings). Entity blocks (knowsAbout/about/mentions) are grounded from a library and never invented; prose (descriptions, audience, serviceOutput) is written by an LLM from "content". For MULTIPLE pages use kg_emit_schema_bulk instead — cheaper to orchestrate and lets pages share a library/business context. Needs a library: pass one inline, reference a just-completed build's libraryPublicId, or a saved + approved library's libraryName (see kg_library_save/kg_library_approve). Returns a runPublicId plus a kg_get poll target. Metered: 500 credits.

pageType.description: Page type driving which schema.org root type is emitted (home/about -> Organization/LocalBusiness, service -> Service-flavored root, blog -> Article).

content.description: Page content to ground the emitted prose (descriptions, audience, serviceOutput, teaches). Provide this or a library reference.

library.description: Inline entity library (from a prior kg_build_library result) to link knowsAbout/about/mentions against.

libraryPublicId.description: Public ID of a previously built library to use instead of an inline library.

libraryName.description: Name of a previously saved + approved library (see kg_library_save/kg_library_approve) to use instead of an inline library.

business.description: Optional business/organization facts (name, address, phone, etc.) to seed the root node.

model.description: Optional OpenRouter model override for schema generation.

whenToUse: Use to emit grounded schema.org JSON-LD for one page from a known library.
avoidWhen: Avoid for multiple pages; use the bulk schema emitter instead.
quality.costSummary: Metered at 500 Thorbit credits for one emitted page.
sideEffects: Creates a durable schema-emission run.
Consumes 500 Thorbit credits upfront.

tools.name: kg_emit_schema_bulk
tools.title: Emit Schema.org JSON-LD (Bulk)
tools.description: Generate schema.org JSON-LD for MULTIPLE pages (up to 200 per call) in one batch, sharing a library/business context unless a page overrides it — use this instead of calling kg_emit_schema in a loop for a batch. Concurrency controls parallelism (default 3, max 8). Returns a runPublicId plus a kg_get poll target. Metered: 500 credits per page.

pages.description: Pages to emit schema.org JSON-LD for, capped at 200 per bulk run.

pageType.description: Page type driving which schema.org root type is emitted (home/about -> Organization/LocalBusiness, service -> Service-flavored root, blog -> Article).

content.description: Page content to ground the emitted prose for this page.

library.description: Optional per-page inline entity library override.

libraryPublicId.description: Optional per-page library public ID override.

libraryName.description: Optional per-page saved library name override.

business.description: Optional business/organization facts shared across all pages in this batch.

library.description: Optional inline entity library shared across all pages that do not override it.

libraryPublicId.description: Optional shared library public ID for pages that do not override it.

libraryName.description: Optional shared saved + approved library name for pages that do not override it.

model.description: Optional OpenRouter model override for schema generation.

concurrency.description: Parallelism for page emission, capped at 8.

whenToUse: Use to emit grounded schema.org JSON-LD for several pages sharing context.
avoidWhen: Avoid for a single page or more than the documented 200-page cap.
quality.costSummary: Metered at 500 Thorbit credits for each page in the batch.
sideEffects: Creates a durable bulk schema-emission run.
Consumes 500 Thorbit credits per page upfront.

tools.name: kg_get
tools.title: Read Knowledge Graph Run Status
tools.description: Poll status, phase, progress, and the artifact manifest for a build or emit run started by kg_build_library, kg_emit_schema, or kg_emit_schema_bulk. This is the ONLY way to retrieve a run's result — the start tools never return the finished library/schema inline. Poll until status is completed or failed.

runPublicId.description: Knowledge Graph run public ID returned by kg_build_library, kg_emit_schema, or kg_emit_schema_bulk.

whenToUse: Use to poll a build or schema-emission run until it is terminal.
avoidWhen: Avoid before a start tool returns a real run public ID.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost read of durable run state and bounded results.

tools.name: kg_library_approve
tools.title: Approve Library
tools.description: Approve (or unapprove, with approved:false) a saved library by name. Required before kg_emit_schema/kg_emit_schema_bulk can reference it via libraryName — an unapproved library still works if passed inline or by libraryPublicId.

name.description: Saved library name to approve (or unapprove).

approved.description: Approval state. A saved library must be approved before kg_emit_schema can reference it by name.

whenToUse: Use to approve or unapprove one exact saved library for named emission.
avoidWhen: Avoid before inspecting the exact saved library and intended approval state.
quality.costSummary: Unmetered caller-organization approval mutation.
sideEffects: Changes whether a saved library may be used by name for schema emission.

tools.name: kg_library_get
tools.title: Read Saved Library
tools.description: Read one saved entity library by exact name, including its approval state. Use kg_library_list first if you don't already know the exact saved name.

whenToUse: Use to inspect one saved library and its approval state by exact name.
avoidWhen: Avoid when the library name is unknown; list libraries first.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost caller-organization library read.

tools.name: kg_library_list
tools.title: List Saved Libraries
tools.description: List saved entity libraries for this org, most recent first. Use this to find a library to reference by name in kg_emit_schema*, or before kg_library_get/kg_library_remove when you don't already know the exact saved name. Pending (unapproved) libraries are excluded unless includePending:true.

includePending.description: When true, include libraries saved but not yet approved.

whenToUse: Use to browse saved libraries or find an exact reusable library name.
avoidWhen: Avoid when the exact library name is already known.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost bounded caller-organization library read.

tools.name: kg_library_remove
tools.title: Remove Library
tools.description: Permanently delete a saved library by name. Destructive and cannot be undone — does not affect libraries already embedded inline in past runs, only future kg_emit_schema*/libraryName lookups by this name.

name.description: Saved library name to remove. This is destructive and cannot be undone.

whenToUse: Use after explicit confirmation to permanently remove one exact saved library.
avoidWhen: Avoid when the library may be needed again or the exact name is uncertain.
quality.costSummary: Unmetered destructive caller-organization library mutation.
sideEffects: Permanently deletes the named saved library and cannot be undone.

tools.name: kg_library_save
tools.title: Save Library
tools.description: Save a completed build's entity library under a name for reuse across kg_emit_schema calls, instead of re-passing it inline every time. Reference the build by libraryPublicId, or pass an inline library directly. Newly saved libraries are UNAPPROVED — kg_emit_schema*/libraryName will reject them until kg_library_approve is called.

name.description: Name to save this library under (org-scoped).

libraryPublicId.description: Public ID of a previously built library to save.

library.description: Inline entity library to save instead of referencing a prior build.

niche.description: Optional niche/category tag for this saved library.

note.description: Optional free-text note about this library.

whenToUse: Use to save a completed or inline entity library under a reusable name.
avoidWhen: Avoid before a build has produced a real library or when reuse is unnecessary.
quality.costSummary: Unmetered caller-organization library mutation.
sideEffects: Persists or updates a named unapproved library for the caller organization.

tools.name: kg_resolve_term
tools.title: Resolve Term
tools.description: Resolve ONE term or phrase (up to 400 chars) to a canonical Wikidata/Wikipedia/DBpedia/Freebase entity, without building a full library. Use this for a quick single lookup; use kg_build_library when you need a whole page's or site's entities linked and structured into a reusable library. Synchronous, unmetered — no polling needed.

term.description: Term or phrase to resolve to a canonical Wikidata/Wikipedia/DBpedia/Freebase entity.

whenToUse: Use to resolve one term to canonical public knowledge-graph identifiers.
avoidWhen: Avoid when a complete reusable entity library is required.
quality.costSummary: Unmetered synchronous knowledge-graph lookup.

tools.name: thorbit_content_harvest_serp
tools.title: Harvest SERP And PAA Evidence
tools.description: Harvest Google SERP/PAA evidence through MCP Scraper: PAA flat questions, PAA tree, organic SERP, local pack, videos/shorts, forums, whatPeopleSaying, AI Overview text/citations/sections, AI Mode, entity IDs, stats, diagnostics, and retry attempts (up to 200 questions via maxQuestions). Split topic from location when possible. Keep proxyMode as location for US city/state SERPs so MCP Scraper rotates fresh residential proxy IDs and browser sessions across retryable CAPTCHA/proxy/location-mismatch failures; pass proxyZip for city-center ZIP targeting. For one specific page instead of search evidence, use thorbit_content_extract_url.

query.description: Core search topic. Separate location when possible, e.g. query="best CRM" and location="Denver, CO".

location.description: Optional search location, such as Denver, CO. Required for precise residential proxy targeting.

gl.description: Optional Google country code, such as us.

hl.description: Optional Google interface language, such as en.

device.description: SERP device context. Use desktop by default; use mobile only when requested.

maxQuestions.description: Maximum PAA questions when serpOnly is false.

includeSerp.description: Include organic SERP results. Default true.

serpOnly.description: Use fast SERP-only mode when PAA expansion is not needed.

proxyMode.description: MCP Scraper proxy mode. Use location by default for US city/state SERPs so MCP Scraper rotates fresh residential proxy IDs and browser sessions across retryable CAPTCHA/proxy/location failures.

proxyZip.description: Optional US ZIP override for residential location proxy targeting. Use when a specific ZIP or city-center ZIP is known.

debug.description: Include sanitized MCP Scraper browser/proxy/location diagnostics and attempt telemetry. Use true when debugging CAPTCHA, proxy selection, or localization.

pages.description: Number of Google result pages to fetch in SERP-only mode.

whenToUse: Use for Google SERP and People Also Ask evidence.
avoidWhen: Avoid for extracting one known URL or researching Reddit conversations.
quality.costSummary: External MCP Scraper search and optional browser work.

tools.name: thorbit_content_opportunities_list
tools.title: List Content Opportunities
tools.description: List persisted content opportunity candidates (from GSC, topic-map, roadmap, ranked keyword, competitor, entity, or question sources) for a project. Use this before starting pipeline work from one of those sources — for raw on-page source options (pages to analyze, not opportunity candidates), use thorbit_onpage_list_sources instead.

projectPublicId.description: Thorbit project public ID.

sourceKind.description: Optional content opportunity source kind filter.

whenToUse: Use to choose a persisted content opportunity for a project.
avoidWhen: Avoid for raw page sources used by On-page analysis.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost caller-organization database read.

tools.name: thorbit_content_optimize
tools.title: Optimize Content From SERP Or Existing Draft
tools.description: High-level content/on-page workflow — the default choice when you don't need explicit mode control. Give it a project, keyword, and either supplied SERP evidence, harvestSerp=true, an existing Thorbit content piece, inline article text, or a URL; it imports raw content when needed, attaches typed SERP evidence, and starts the durable pipeline in optimize mode when content exists or write mode when only SERP evidence exists. For explicit brief/write/optimize mode control instead of automatic selection, use thorbit_content_pipeline_start. Returns a jobPublicId plus a thorbit_content_pipeline_get poll target.

projectPublicId.description: Thorbit project public ID.

keyword.description: Target keyword/query for optimization or SERP-guided content creation.

content.description: Existing content to optimize: Thorbit content piece, inline text/markdown, or public URL. Omit when creating new content from SERP evidence only.

contentPiecePublicId.description: Existing Thorbit content piece public ID to optimize.

title.description: Optional title for the imported draft content.

text.description: Existing article or page content to import into Thorbit and optimize. Markdown or plain text are accepted.

sourceUrl.description: Optional canonical/source URL for the imported content.

url.description: Public page URL to extract with MCP Scraper, import as a Thorbit draft, and optimize.

browserFallback.description: Use MCP Scraper browser fallback for JS-heavy pages.

extractBranding.description: Ask MCP Scraper to extract branding metadata when supported.

downloadMedia.description: Ask MCP Scraper to download page media when supported.

maxCharacters.description: Maximum extracted characters imported into the optimization draft.

serpEvidence.description: Optional SERP evidence object, usually returned by thorbit_content_harvest_serp.

query.description: Query used to collect this SERP evidence.

organicResults.description: Organic SERP results, usually from thorbit_content_harvest_serp.

whatPeopleSaying.description: What people are saying or discussion cards.

harvestSerp.description: When true, Thorbit will harvest SERP/PAA evidence through MCP Scraper before starting the optimization workflow.

location.description: Optional SERP location for harvestSerp, such as Denver, CO.

gl.description: Optional Google country code for harvestSerp, such as us.

hl.description: Optional Google interface language for harvestSerp, such as en.

proxyMode.description: MCP Scraper proxy mode for harvestSerp. Keep location for local US SERPs.

proxyZip.description: Optional US ZIP override for residential proxy targeting.

debug.description: Include sanitized MCP Scraper retry/proxy diagnostics when harvestSerp is true.

pages.description: Google pages to fetch for SERP-only style evidence.

reviewBrief.description: Pause after the generated brief before writing/optimization continues.

notes.description: Optional optimization instructions for the content pipeline.

annotations.title: Optimize Content From SERP Or Existing Draft

whenToUse: Use when Thorbit should choose write or optimize from supplied evidence.
avoidWhen: Avoid when explicit brief, write, or optimize mode control is required.
quality.costSummary: Metered durable content workflow with provider and model usage.
sideEffects: Creates or updates durable content workflow state.

tools.name: thorbit_content_pipeline_artifact_read
tools.title: Read Content Pipeline Artifact
tools.description: Read ONE blob artifact from a content pipeline job by id (article, brief, briefJson, analysis, plan, verification, projectContext) — use this instead of pulling the whole job via thorbit_content_pipeline_get when you only need the finished article or brief text. Ids correspond to the *BlobPath fields surfaced by thorbit_content_pipeline_get. Returns a small inline preview by default (maxBytes default 2,000, truncated with a flag) plus a permanent blobUrl link to the full content — do not raise maxBytes to dump the whole thing into context by default; offer the user the link, or explicitly raise maxBytes only when you genuinely need more inline.

jobPublicId.description: Content pipeline workflow job public ID.

artifactId.description: Which blob artifact to read. article = final article markdown; brief = writer brief; analysis = on-page analysis bundle.

maxBytes.description: Max bytes of content to return inline; content is truncated with a flag if larger. Response always includes blobUrl regardless of this cap — raise maxBytes on a follow-up call, or fetch blobUrl directly, to get the rest.

whenToUse: Use to read one known pipeline artifact with a bounded inline preview.
avoidWhen: Avoid for general job status or when the artifact identifier is unknown.
quality.costSummary: Bounded caller-organization artifact read.

tools.name: thorbit_content_pipeline_get
tools.title: Read Content Pipeline
tools.description: Poll a content pipeline job's phase, next actions, brief/article markdown, writer sections, model call telemetry, and publication summary. This is the ONLY way to check progress — start*/optimize/improve tools return immediately, before the pipeline finishes. For one specific artifact instead of the whole job view, use thorbit_content_pipeline_artifact_read.

jobPublicId.description: Content pipeline workflow job public ID.

includePhaseData.description: Include raw workflow phaseData in addition to the normalized run view.

whenToUse: Use to poll one durable content pipeline job.
avoidWhen: Avoid when only one completed blob artifact is needed.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost caller-organization workflow status read.

tools.name: thorbit_content_pipeline_improve
tools.title: Improve Existing Content
tools.description: Start an improvement loop for an EXISTING content pipeline job or content piece — scores it, identifies gaps, rewrites, and re-scores. Use this instead of thorbit_content_optimize/thorbit_content_pipeline_start when you're not starting fresh but iterating on something already written.

jobOrPiecePublicId.description: Existing content pipeline job public ID or content piece public ID to improve.

whenToUse: Use to improve an existing pipeline job or content piece.
avoidWhen: Avoid when starting a new article from a keyword.
quality.costSummary: Metered durable score, rewrite, and verification workflow.
sideEffects: Creates an improvement workflow for existing content.

tools.name: thorbit_content_pipeline_resume
tools.title: Resume Content Pipeline
tools.description: Resume a content pipeline job that thorbit_content_pipeline_get reported as paused (pausePoint=strategy|brief) after strategy or brief review, optionally appending userInstructions before the next phase dispatch. Only valid when a poll shows a pause point — calling it otherwise has no effect.

jobPublicId.description: Paused content pipeline workflow job public ID.

userInstructions.description: Optional instructions to append before resuming.

whenToUse: Use only after a pipeline reports a strategy or brief pause.
avoidWhen: Avoid for running, completed, failed, or unpaused jobs.
quality.costSummary: Metered workflow transition that resumes asynchronous execution.
sideEffects: Resumes a paused durable content pipeline.

tools.name: thorbit_content_pipeline_start
tools.title: Start Content Pipeline
tools.description: Start the content pipeline in brief, write, or optimize mode with explicit control over which mode runs. Supports persisted opportunity sources, approved project context, writing style IDs, brief review pauses (reviewBrief), and existing content optimization. Prefer thorbit_content_optimize instead when you don't need to force a specific mode — it picks write vs optimize automatically. Metered, durable.

projectPublicId.description: Thorbit project public ID.

keyword.description: Target keyword or query for the content pipeline.

mode.description: Content pipeline mode: brief, write, or optimize.

reviewBrief.description: Pause after brief generation for review before writing.

existingContentPiecePublicId.description: Required for optimize mode. Existing Thorbit content piece public ID.

analysisPublicId.description: Optional related on-page analysis public ID.

source.description: Optional persisted content opportunity source reference.

whenToUse: Use to start a durable pipeline with explicit mode control.
avoidWhen: Avoid when automatic write versus optimize selection is preferred.
quality.costSummary: Metered durable content workflow with asynchronous execution.
sideEffects: Creates a durable content pipeline job.

tools.name: thorbit_content_pipeline_start_from_brief
tools.title: Start Writing From Brief
tools.description: Start the write pipeline directly from an already-approved brief and its on-page analysis — use this specifically after thorbit_onpage_generate_brief has produced a brief you're happy with. For starting from a keyword/SERP/existing draft instead of a pre-made brief, use thorbit_content_optimize or thorbit_content_pipeline_start.

analysisPublicId.description: On-page analysis public ID associated with the brief.

whenToUse: Use to write directly from an approved On-page brief.
avoidWhen: Avoid when no approved brief and analysis identifiers exist.
quality.costSummary: Metered durable writing workflow from an approved brief.
sideEffects: Creates a durable content pipeline job.

tools.name: thorbit_content_reddit_research
tools.title: Research Reddit With MCP Scraper Browser Agent
tools.description: Find Reddit candidates through MCP Scraper SERP harvest, then read up to 10 selected posts (maxPosts) through MCP Scraper browser-agent by default. Use for authentic audience language, objections, pain points, and questions — do NOT use thorbit_content_extract_url or generic scraping for Reddit, it needs the browser-agent path. Keep proxyMode as location and pass location/proxyZip when the research has a local market.

query.description: Topic, product, service, or pain point to research on Reddit.

location.description: Optional location to bias MCP Scraper SERP discovery and residential proxy targeting.

gl.description: Optional Google country code, such as us.

hl.description: Optional Google interface language, such as en.

device.description: SERP device context for Reddit discovery.

proxyMode.description: MCP Scraper proxy mode for Reddit discovery. Use location by default so MCP Scraper owns CAPTCHA/proxy retries.

proxyZip.description: Optional US ZIP override for residential location proxy targeting.

debug.description: Include sanitized MCP Scraper retry/proxy diagnostics for Reddit discovery.

maxPosts.description: Maximum Reddit posts to read with MCP Scraper browser-agent.

readWithBrowserAgent.description: Keep true. Reads Reddit candidates through MCP Scraper browser-agent.

profile.description: Optional MCP Scraper browser-agent saved profile name.

whenToUse: Use for Reddit audience language, pain points, and objections.
avoidWhen: Avoid for generic web pages or Google-only search evidence.
quality.costSummary: External SERP discovery plus bounded browser-agent reading.

tools.name: thorbit_deposition_artifact_read
tools.title: Read Depositioning Artifact
tools.description: Read ONE artifact from a run's folder by id (e.g. research/own.json, research/competitor-2.json, vulnerability.json, playbook.md) — use this instead of pulling the whole run when you only need one piece of evidence. Artifact ids come from thorbit_deposition_get's manifest. For the finished composed playbook itself, use thorbit_deposition_get_playbook instead. Returns a small inline preview by default (maxBytes default 2,000, capped at 500,000) and preserves the artifact URI for complete content. A truncated response does not advertise a repeat read because no larger public inline response is available.

artifactId.description: Artifact id from the run manifest, e.g. "research/own.json", "research/competitor-2.json", "vulnerability.json", "playbook.md". Get ids from thorbit_deposition_get.

maxBytes.description: Maximum content bytes to return inline, capped at the public 500000-byte response limit. When truncated, use the returned artifact URI for the complete content.

whenToUse: Use to read one known research or playbook artifact with a bounded inline preview.
avoidWhen: Avoid for run status, artifact discovery, or the composed playbook summary.
quality.costSummary: Bounded caller-organization artifact read with a caller-selected byte cap.

tools.name: thorbit_deposition_get
tools.title: Read Depositioning Run Status
tools.description: Poll status, phase, progress, selected binding state and strategy, primary vulnerability, category class, displacement mechanism, and whether the playbook is ready for a run started by thorbit_deposition_start. This is the ONLY way to check progress — thorbit_deposition_start returns immediately, before the pipeline finishes. Poll until status is complete or failed; leave includePhaseData off unless you need the whole raw per-phase bundle.

runPublicId.description: Deposition run public ID returned by thorbit_deposition_start.

includePhaseData.description: Include raw per-phase intermediate data in addition to the lean status.

whenToUse: Use to poll one known run and inspect progress, terminal state, and artifact inventory.
avoidWhen: Avoid for the finished playbook or one known artifact after the run completes.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost caller-organization durable-run status read.

tools.name: thorbit_deposition_get_playbook
tools.title: Read Depositioning Playbook
tools.description: Return the finished strategy playbook markdown for a completed run (executive brief, the four elements, activation guide). Check thorbit_deposition_get first: if the playbook is not ready, this returns an isError not_found result; poll thorbit_deposition_get until the run completes before retrying. For one specific research artifact instead of the composed playbook, use thorbit_deposition_artifact_read.

runPublicId.description: Deposition run public ID. Returns the markdown playbook once the run is complete.

whenToUse: Use after completion to read the composed positioning strategy playbook.
avoidWhen: Avoid while the run is active or when one specific research artifact is needed.
quality.costSummary: Bounded caller-organization completed-playbook read.

tools.name: thorbit_deposition_list
tools.title: List Depositioning Runs
tools.description: List past Depositioning runs (most recent first) for a project or the whole org, with company, category, status, binding state, and strategy. Use this to browse/find a prior run when you already know roughly which project or company; for a text search across run content and strategy topics, use thorbit_deposition_search instead.

projectPublicId.description: Optional project filter; omit for all org runs.

whenToUse: Use to browse recent or status-filtered Deposition runs for a project or organization.
avoidWhen: Avoid for topic or content matching across runs; use full-text search instead.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost bounded caller-organization run listing.

tools.name: thorbit_deposition_search
tools.title: Search Depositioning Runs
tools.description: Full-text search across past Depositioning runs — matches the query against company, category, and playbook content, not just company name. Use this when looking for prior strategy work by topic (e.g. "pricing opacity", "switching cost") rather than browsing recent activity (see thorbit_deposition_list).

query.description: Text to search across run company, category, and playbook content.

projectPublicId.description: Optional project filter.

whenToUse: Use to search company, category, and playbook content across prior runs.
avoidWhen: Avoid for browsing recent or active runs; use the bounded list tool instead.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost bounded caller-organization full-text run search.

tools.name: thorbit_deposition_start
tools.title: Start Depositioning Run
tools.description: Start a durable Depositioning strategy run for a challenger product in a category — researches competitors and customers, finds the binding vulnerability, classifies movers, designs a category class, builds a displacement mechanism, and writes a playbook. competitorUrls are auto-discovered via SERP if you give fewer than 2. Pass `context` when the website is generic or the real positioning isn't obvious — it steers research, competitor discovery, vulnerability, and category ownership as authoritative ground truth. Returns a runPublicId plus a thorbit_deposition_get poll target — the playbook itself is not returned inline; call thorbit_deposition_get_playbook once complete. Metered.

companyName.description: The challenger company or product being depositioned.

productUrl.description: URL of the challenger product homepage.

categoryName.description: The product category, e.g. "B2B sales analytics".

competitorUrls.description: 0-5 competitor URLs. Auto-discovered via SERP if fewer than 2 are provided.

reviewsUrl.description: Optional customer reviews URL (G2, Trustpilot, Reddit thread).

knownPains.description: Optional known customer pain points to seed the analysis.

context.description: Optional free-form context about the company that gets passed to the AI as authoritative ground truth — its real niche, target audience, monetization, founder/standard-bearer, beliefs/mission, and who its true competitors are. Use this when the website is generic or the real positioning is not obvious from the homepage. When provided it steers research, competitor discovery, vulnerability, and category ownership.

projectPublicId.description: Optional Thorbit project to associate the run with.

whenToUse: Use to start a durable strategy run for a challenger product and category.
avoidWhen: Avoid when reading or finding an existing run; use the status, list, or search tools.
quality.costSummary: Metered durable research and strategy workflow with external provider and model usage.
sideEffects: Creates a durable Deposition run and consumes caller-organization credits.

tools.name: thorbit_icp_artifact_read
tools.title: Read ICP Artifact
tools.description: Read one persisted ICP artifact from the Phoenix manifest. Content is bounded to 500000 public characters. This package does not recreate Mastra workflow data.

artifactId.description: Artifact id from the run manifest, e.g. "final_icp", "eos_framework", "research_notes", "reddit_insights". Get ids from thorbit_icp_get.

maxBytes.description: Maximum inline artifact content bytes, capped at the public 500000-character result limit.

whenToUse: Use to read one exact persisted artifact named by the completed ICP run manifest.
avoidWhen: Avoid for the composed semantic result or when the artifact ID is not known.
quality.costSummary: Bounded Phoenix artifact read capped at 500,000 public characters.

tools.name: thorbit_icp_get
tools.title: Read ICP Run Status
tools.description: Poll Phoenix-projected status, phase, progress, result readiness, safe failure details, and artifacts for Mastra-backed ICP execution. A completed result points to thorbit_icp_get_result and persisted artifacts.

runPublicId.description: Phoenix ICP run public ID returned by thorbit_icp_start.

detail.description: Status verbosity: summary, standard (adds artifact manifest), or full (adds per-phase summaries).

includePhaseData.description: Include raw per-phase intermediate data; admin/debug only.

whenToUse: Use to poll Phoenix-projected progress and result readiness for one known ICP run.
avoidWhen: Avoid before a start, list, or search tool supplies a real Phoenix run public ID.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost synchronous read of Phoenix-persisted status and at most 100 artifact references.

tools.name: thorbit_icp_get_result
tools.title: Read ICP Result
tools.description: Return a bounded projection of a completed Mastra-backed ICP from the Phoenix control plane, with primary segment, segments, buying triggers, objections, and the real final_icp artifact reference. If the result is not persisted in Phoenix, returns an isError not_found result with a thorbit_icp_get poll action.

runPublicId.description: Phoenix ICP run public ID. Returns the composed ICP document once the Mastra-backed run is complete.

format.description: markdown reads the composed ICP content; json reads the structured final artifact.

maxBytes.description: Maximum provider content bytes used to create the bounded public ICP projection.

whenToUse: Use to read the bounded semantic projection after ICP status reports completion.
avoidWhen: Avoid while execution is active; poll status until Phoenix reports the result ready.
quality.costSummary: Bounded Phoenix result read using caller-selected maxBytes capped at 1,000,000.

tools.name: thorbit_icp_list
tools.title: List ICP Runs
tools.description: List up to 100 Phoenix-tracked, Mastra-backed ICP runs for the caller organization, optionally filtered by project, target text, or status. Phoenix supplies the persisted job projection; this package does not query the execution runtime directly.

projectPublicId.description: Optional project filter; omit for all org runs.

whenToUse: Use to browse recent caller-organization ICP runs or filter them by project and status.
avoidWhen: Avoid for subject or content matching; use ICP search instead.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost paginated Phoenix read capped at 100 runs per request.

tools.name: thorbit_icp_search
tools.title: Search ICP Runs
tools.description: Search Phoenix-persisted content from caller-organization, Mastra-backed ICP runs and return at most 50 provider-backed matches. The package does not fabricate relevance scores, timestamps, runtime identities, or pagination certainty.

query.description: Text to search across run target, project, and ICP content.

projectPublicId.description: Optional project filter.

whenToUse: Use to search persisted ICP targets, projects, and result content by subject.
avoidWhen: Avoid for browsing recent activity without a query; use the ICP run list.
quality.costSummary: Bounded paginated Phoenix search capped at 50 persisted matches per request.

tools.name: thorbit_icp_start
tools.title: Start ICP Run
tools.description: Start Mastra-backed ICP execution through the Phoenix control plane for a caller-organization project. Phoenix owns authentication, the durable runPublicId, metering, and persisted state; Mastra owns execution. Success requires accepted runtime dispatch and never invents a runtime or job identity. Returns a thorbit_icp_get poll target.

projectPublicId.description: Thorbit project public ID. Required for v1 production MCP runs.

input.description: Business URL, brand description, or audience descriptor. Defaults to the project URL/domain when omitted.

skipResearch.description: When true, generate from existing context without external research.

maxResearchRounds.description: Bounded research bursts. Each burst is one short workflow step.

serpConcurrency.description: MCP Scraper SERP concurrency; capped at 50.

idempotencyKey.description: Optional key; a matching active/completed run is returned instead of starting a duplicate.

whenToUse: Use to start a new Ideal Customer Profile workflow for one caller-organization project.
avoidWhen: Avoid when a run already exists; poll, list, or search before starting duplicate work.
quality.costSummary: Metered Mastra execution with at most three research rounds and SERP concurrency capped at 50.
sideEffects: Creates a durable caller-organization ICP run in the Phoenix control plane.
Dispatches Mastra execution and records metered provider usage for the caller organization.

tools.name: thorbit_kb_ask
tools.title: Ask Thorbit Knowledge Base
tools.description: Answer a question using only retrieved Knowledge Base context and return a bounded answer, citations, follow-ups, and nullable real model ID. Use extractive style for excerpts; use thorbit_kb_search for raw scored chunks. This may invoke a metered answer model.

question.description: Question to answer using Thorbit KB context only.

knowledgeBasePublicId.description: Optional target KB. Omit to answer over visible KBs.

projectPublicId.description: Optional project scope when no KB is specified.

answerStyle.description: Use extractive to return source excerpts without an LLM answer.

limit.description: Maximum retrieved chunks used for the answer.

requireCitations.description: Keep true for grounded answers with citation arrays.

whenToUse: Use to synthesize a direct answer grounded in bounded Knowledge Base citations.
avoidWhen: Avoid when raw retrieved passages are required for caller-side reasoning.
quality.costSummary: Bounded retrieval plus potentially metered model answer generation.

tools.name: thorbit_kb_create
tools.title: Create Thorbit Knowledge Base
tools.description: Create a new vector-backed knowledge base for ingestion, RAG search, and grounded Q&A. Org-level by default; pass projectPublicId to scope it to one project. Use thorbit_kb_list first if you're not sure whether a suitable knowledge base already exists.

name.description: Name for the new Thorbit knowledge base.

description.description: Optional description for the new knowledge base.

projectPublicId.description: Optional Thorbit project public ID. Omit to create an org-level knowledge base.

folder.description: Optional organizational folder, such as research or domains.

whenToUse: Use to create a new caller-organization or project Knowledge Base.
avoidWhen: Avoid when a suitable Knowledge Base already exists; list first.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost durable Knowledge Base record creation.
sideEffects: Creates a durable caller-organization Knowledge Base.

tools.name: thorbit_kb_ingest_site
tools.title: Ingest Website Into Thorbit KB
tools.description: Map a website through MCP Scraper, extract selected pages (up to 100, default 25), and vectorize them. For one known page, use thorbit_kb_ingest_url because it is cheaper and faster. The exact public receipt succeeds only when Phoenix supplies one unambiguous source; multi-source provider batches fail closed rather than hiding source IDs.

startUrl.description: Website start URL. Thorbit maps URLs through MCP Scraper, then ingests selected pages.

includePatterns.description: Optional regex or substring patterns that a mapped URL must match.

excludePatterns.description: Optional regex or substring patterns that remove mapped URLs.

maxPages.description: Maximum pages to ingest. Default 25, hard cap 100.

mode.description: Append source versions. Refresh mode is intentionally not enabled in V1.

metadata.description: Optional metadata stored on each KB source.

whenToUse: Use to map and ingest a bounded set of pages from one website.
avoidWhen: Avoid for one known URL, which is cheaper through URL ingestion.
userPhrases: build a KB from this site
quality.costSummary: Bounded MCP Scraper mapping and extraction plus durable vectorization per page.
sideEffects: Creates append-only Knowledge Base sources and chunks for accepted pages.

tools.name: thorbit_kb_ingest_text
tools.title: Ingest Text Into Thorbit KB
tools.description: Submit bounded text or Markdown already in hand directly into a knowledge base with no scraping. Use thorbit_kb_ingest_url when content must be fetched. Returns the real source receipt and a thorbit_kb_source_status follow-up after durable chunking and vectorization.

title.description: Source title shown in KB citations.

content.description: Text or Markdown to chunk, embed, and ingest.

sourceUrl.description: Optional canonical source URL for citations.

sourceType.description: Caller-facing source type. Thorbit stores note/markdown as manual sources in V1.

metadata.description: Optional metadata stored on the KB source.

whenToUse: Use to ingest text or Markdown already supplied by the caller.
avoidWhen: Avoid when Thorbit must fetch a URL, website, or YouTube transcript.
userPhrases: add these notes to the KB
quality.costSummary: Durable chunking and vectorization without external scraping.
sideEffects: Creates an append-only durable Knowledge Base source and chunks.

tools.name: thorbit_kb_ingest_url
tools.title: Ingest URL Into Thorbit KB
tools.description: Extract ONE public URL through MCP Scraper, clean it, and vectorize it into a knowledge base (up to 500,000 chars before chunking). For a whole site instead of one page, use thorbit_kb_ingest_site. Append-only: re-ingesting adds a new source version. Returns a real source receipt and thorbit_kb_source_status follow-up.

url.description: Public URL to extract through MCP Scraper and ingest into Thorbit.

title.description: Optional title override. Provider title is used when omitted.

mode.description: Append a new source version. Refresh mode is intentionally not enabled in V1.

metadata.description: Optional metadata stored on the KB source.

whenToUse: Use to extract and ingest one public URL into a known Knowledge Base.
avoidWhen: Avoid for whole websites, YouTube videos, or content already in hand.
userPhrases: add this page to the KB
quality.costSummary: External MCP Scraper extraction plus durable chunking and vectorization.
sideEffects: Creates an append-only durable Knowledge Base source and chunks.

tools.name: thorbit_kb_ingest_youtube
tools.title: Ingest YouTube Into Thorbit KB
tools.description: Transcribe one YouTube video through MCP Scraper and vectorize the transcript, preserving timestamp chunks by default. For web pages or raw text, use thorbit_kb_ingest_url or thorbit_kb_ingest_text. Returns the real source receipt and a thorbit_kb_source_status follow-up.

videoUrl.description: YouTube video URL. Thorbit transcribes through MCP Scraper and vectorizes transcript chunks.

preserveTimestamps.description: Store transcript timestamps on chunks when MCP Scraper returns them.

metadata.description: Optional metadata stored on the KB source.

whenToUse: Use to transcribe and ingest one YouTube video into a known Knowledge Base.
avoidWhen: Avoid for web pages or caller-supplied text.
userPhrases: add this transcript to the KB
quality.costSummary: External transcription plus durable chunking and vectorization.
sideEffects: Creates an append-only durable transcript source and chunks.

tools.name: thorbit_kb_list
tools.title: List Thorbit Knowledge Bases
tools.description: List knowledge bases visible to this API key, org-level and project-scoped. Use before ingestion/search when you don't already have the target knowledgeBasePublicId — every ingest tool needs one (search/ask can omit it to query all visible KBs instead).

projectPublicId.description: Optional Thorbit project public ID used to narrow knowledge-base listing.

includeGlobal.description: Include org-level/global knowledge bases. Default true.

whenToUse: Use to find a visible Knowledge Base public ID before ingestion or retrieval.
avoidWhen: Avoid when the exact Knowledge Base public ID is already known.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost caller-organization database read.

tools.name: thorbit_kb_search
tools.title: Search Thorbit Knowledge Base
tools.description: Search visible knowledge-base content and return at most 50 bounded, scored chunks plus bounded provider citations, without synthesizing an answer. Use thorbit_kb_ask for a direct grounded response. Omit knowledgeBasePublicId to search visible KBs.

query.description: Search query. Use the user question or a focused retrieval query.

knowledgeBasePublicId.description: Optional target KB. Omit to search visible KBs.

projectPublicId.description: Optional project scope when no KB is specified.

searchMode.description: smart uses Thorbit intent/rerank retrieval; hybrid uses ANN plus FTS.

whenToUse: Use to retrieve bounded citation-ready chunks without answer synthesis.
avoidWhen: Avoid when the caller wants a direct grounded answer.
quality.costSummary: Bounded vector or hybrid retrieval and optional reranking.

tools.name: thorbit_kb_source_status
tools.title: Read Thorbit KB Source Status
tools.description: Poll ingestion status for a source public ID returned by a thorbit_kb_ingest_* tool. Returns the real source state, progress, safe error, and updated time; continue polling until ready or failed, then use thorbit_kb_search or thorbit_kb_ask.

sourcePublicIds.description: Source public IDs returned by Thorbit KB ingestion tools.

whenToUse: Use to poll one provider-backed source receipt returned by ingestion.
avoidWhen: Avoid for retrieving chunks or synthesizing an answer.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost caller-organization source status read.

tools.name: thorbit_money_kw_get
tools.title: Read Money Keyword Run Status
tools.description: Poll status, current gate, and whether the tiered keyword list is ready (targetsReady) for a run started by thorbit_money_kw_start. This is the ONLY way to check progress — thorbit_money_kw_start returns immediately, before the run finishes. Poll until status is completed or failed.

runPublicId.description: Money Keyword run public ID returned by thorbit_money_kw_start.

whenToUse: Use to poll status and readiness for an existing Money Keyword research run.
avoidWhen: Avoid starting duplicate research or reading completed targets directly.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost synchronous caller-organization status read.

tools.name: thorbit_money_kw_get_targets
tools.title: Read Money Keyword List
tools.description: Return the tiered "money keyword" list for a completed run. Each target has a tier (Quick Win / Builder / Flagship), a track (Now / Next / Verify / Later), a proven flag (evidence-confirmed), difficulty, and a url slug. Check thorbit_money_kw_get first: targets are only ready once status is completed.

limit.description: Maximum tiered keyword targets to return.

whenToUse: Use to retrieve the validated tiered target list from a completed research run.
avoidWhen: Avoid before the status reader reports that keyword targets are ready.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost synchronous caller-organization target read.

tools.name: thorbit_money_kw_start
tools.title: Start Money Keyword Run
tools.description: Start a durable native-Mastra compact-keyword research run for one or more company/offer names — OpenRouter MiniMax 3 and MCP Scraper evidence. companyNames is required; websiteUrl, rootEntity, centralIntent, competitors, and seedTopics steer the research. Use idempotencyKey to retry safely. Returns a Phoenix-owned runPublicId plus a thorbit_money_kw_get poll target — the tiered list is not returned inline; call thorbit_money_kw_get_targets once complete. Metered.

companyNames.description: One or more company/offer names to research.

websiteUrl.description: Optional canonical business website used for owned-site evidence.

rootEntity.description: Optional root entity/product the offer centers on.

centralIntent.description: Optional central commercial intent of the offer.

competitors.description: Optional known competitor names (allowed in keywords).

seedTopics.description: Optional seed topics to steer research.

idempotencyKey.description: Optional idempotency key for safely retrying the same start request.

whenToUse: Use to start durable commercial keyword research for one or more companies or offers.
avoidWhen: Avoid when checking an existing run; use the Money Keyword status reader.
quality.costSummary: Metered asynchronous Mastra research using model and MCP Scraper provider calls.
sideEffects: Creates a durable Mastra-backed Money Keyword research run.
Records metered research usage for the caller organization.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_apply_edits
tools.title: Apply On-Page Edits
tools.description: Apply all ACCEPTED edits (from thorbit_onpage_update_edit_status) to the editable content piece and create before/after version snapshots — the final step of the edit loop. Rescore afterward with thorbit_onpage_rescore_analysis to see the impact.

analysisPublicId.description: On-page analysis public ID with accepted edits.

whenToUse: Use to apply all accepted edits and create version snapshots.
avoidWhen: Avoid before the user has reviewed and accepted exact proposals.
quality.costSummary: Content mutation that writes accepted edits and versions.
sideEffects: Mutates editable content and creates before and after versions.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_generate_brief
tools.title: Generate On-Page Brief
tools.description: Return an existing writer brief immediately, or queue brief generation for a completed on-page analysis (poll with thorbit_onpage_get_analysis). Persists brief content and structured data for later writing — feed the result into thorbit_content_pipeline_start_from_brief. For the separate strategy document instead of a writing brief, use thorbit_onpage_generate_strategy.

analysisPublicId.description: Completed on-page analysis public ID. Returns an existing brief immediately, or queues generation and returns a job ID plus a thorbit_onpage_get_analysis poll target.

regenerate.description: Regenerate an existing brief instead of returning it. Regeneration queues work and should be polled with thorbit_onpage_get_analysis.

whenToUse: Use to create or retrieve a writer brief from completed analysis evidence.
avoidWhen: Avoid when a strategy document rather than a writer brief is required.
quality.costSummary: Potentially metered document generation from stored analysis.
sideEffects: Persists a writer brief when generation is required.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_generate_strategy
tools.title: Generate On-Page Strategy
tools.description: Generate and persist the on-page strategy document for a completed analysis, optionally using article content as context. For the writer brief that feeds thorbit_content_pipeline_start_from_brief instead, use thorbit_onpage_generate_brief.

articleContent.description: Optional article content to include in strategy generation.

whenToUse: Use to generate the separate On-page strategy document.
avoidWhen: Avoid when a writer brief for article production is required.
quality.costSummary: Metered strategy generation from stored analysis.
sideEffects: Persists an On-page strategy document.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_get_analysis
tools.title: Read Thorbit On-Page Analysis
tools.description: Poll status, score, signal counts, brief, strategy, and editor state for an analysis started by thorbit_onpage_start_analysis; use detail:"full" for SERP, competitors, topic/demand clusters, Reddit/YouTube, entities, PMI, scoring, content reports, proposed edits, and raw analysisData. This is the ONLY way to check progress — thorbit_onpage_start_analysis returns immediately. For the editable content itself rather than the analysis, use thorbit_onpage_get_editor_content.

analysisPublicId.description: On-page analysis public ID returned by thorbit_onpage_start_analysis.

detail.description: Analysis detail level. Use full for SERP, competitors, clusters, entities, demand, brief, strategy, and raw analysis data.

includeBrief.description: Include persisted brief content and structured brief data when available.

includeStrategy.description: Include persisted strategy content when available.

includeRawAnalysisData.description: Include raw analysisData JSON. Automatically included for detail=full.

whenToUse: Use to poll or inspect one known On-page analysis.
avoidWhen: Avoid when the editable draft content itself is required.
quality.costSummary: Caller-organization analysis status and evidence read.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_get_editor_content
tools.title: Read On-Page Editor Content
tools.description: Read or materialize the EDITABLE content draft for a completed on-page analysis (creates one from the selected stored source if it doesn't exist yet) — returns content piece ID, text, word count, source URL, and stale-score state. For the analysis results themselves (score, gaps, clusters) rather than editable text, use thorbit_onpage_get_analysis instead.

whenToUse: Use to read the editable content associated with an analysis.
avoidWhen: Avoid for scores, evidence, competitors, or analysis status.
quality.costSummary: Bounded caller-organization content read.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_list_analyses
tools.title: List Past On-Page Analyses
tools.description: List a project's previously run on-page analyses (most recent first) with analysisPublicId, keyword, status, overallScore, source, and linked pipeline job. Use to find a past run's analysisPublicId so you can re-query it with thorbit_onpage_get_analysis or build a report — this only lists metadata, use thorbit_onpage_get_analysis for the actual analysis content. Filter by keyword (search) or status.

projectPublicId.description: Thorbit project public ID.

whenToUse: Use to find a previous On-page analysis identifier.
avoidWhen: Avoid when the analysis identifier is already known.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost paginated caller-organization read.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_list_sources
tools.title: List On-Page Source Options
tools.description: List source options that can feed on-page analysis: keyword-only, WordPress Plugin pages, WordPress API synced pages, and project website scrape pages. Use before thorbit_onpage_start_analysis when you need to pick a stored page source. For persisted content-opportunity candidates (GSC/topic-map/competitor sources) instead of raw pages, use thorbit_content_opportunities_list.

projectPublicId.description: Thorbit project public ID.

search.description: Optional search string for page/title/url filtering.

connectionPublicId.description: Optional WordPress connection public ID filter.

whenToUse: Use to choose a stored source for On-page analysis.
avoidWhen: Avoid for persisted content opportunity candidates.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost caller-organization source read.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_propose_edits
tools.title: Propose On-Page Edits
tools.description: Propose 3-8 targeted content edits from the completed analysis gaps and editable content — the first step of the edit loop. Persists a pending edit session; accept/reject each with thorbit_onpage_update_edit_status, then apply with thorbit_onpage_apply_edits.

analysisPublicId.description: Completed full-mode on-page analysis public ID.

whenToUse: Use to propose targeted edits from completed analysis gaps.
avoidWhen: Avoid before analysis completion or without editable content.
quality.costSummary: Metered edit proposal generation.
sideEffects: Persists a pending edit proposal session.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_rescore_analysis
tools.title: Re-Score On-Page Content
tools.description: Re-score a completed analysis against the current editable content piece WITHOUT re-running expensive SERP and competitor collection — use this after edits instead of thorbit_onpage_start_analysis, which always re-collects from scratch. Returns a rescore job ID; poll with thorbit_onpage_get_analysis.

editorContentPiecePublicId.description: Editable content piece public ID returned by thorbit_onpage_get_editor_content.

contentPiecePublicId.description: Alternate content piece public ID to score.

whenToUse: Use after edits to re-score against existing collected evidence.
avoidWhen: Avoid for a first analysis that still needs SERP collection.
quality.costSummary: Metered durable re-score without new SERP collection.
sideEffects: Creates a durable On-page re-score run.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_start_analysis
tools.title: Start Thorbit On-Page Analysis
tools.description: Start an on-page analysis for a project — keyword-only, inline content, an existing Thorbit content piece, WordPress Plugin/API pages, or a project website scrape page. Hosted Thorbit resolves source content, infers keywords when possible, and dispatches the durable analysis workflow. To re-score already-analyzed content after edits instead of starting over, use thorbit_onpage_rescore_analysis — it skips the expensive SERP/competitor collection this tool always runs. Metered.

projectPublicId.description: Thorbit project public ID.

keyword.description: Target keyword or query. Required for keyword-only and inline-content analysis; can be inferred for selected stored sources.

force.description: Force restart if an analysis is already running.

source.description: Source to analyze: keyword_only, inline_content, content_piece, wordpress_plugin_page, wordpress_api_page, or project_website_scrape.

whenToUse: Use to start a new analysis from a keyword or selected content source.
avoidWhen: Avoid when re-scoring edited content from an existing analysis.
quality.costSummary: Metered durable SERP, competitor, and content analysis.
sideEffects: Creates a durable On-page analysis run.

tools.name: thorbit_onpage_update_edit_status
tools.title: Accept Or Reject On-Page Edit
tools.description: Accept or reject ONE proposed edit from a thorbit_onpage_propose_edits session — the middle step of the edit loop. Edits are not written to the content piece until thorbit_onpage_apply_edits runs afterward.

analysisPublicId.description: On-page analysis public ID with a proposed edit session.

status.description: Accept or reject this proposed edit.

whenToUse: Use to accept or reject one exact proposed edit.
avoidWhen: Avoid before edit proposals exist or for applying accepted edits.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost caller-organization edit status mutation.
sideEffects: Changes one persisted edit decision.

tools.name: thorbit_topic_map_artifact_read
tools.title: Read Topic Map Artifact
tools.description: Read ONE artifact from a run by artifact id — use this instead of pulling the whole run when you only need one piece of evidence. Artifact ids come from thorbit_topic_map_get's manifest. For the finished composed map itself, use thorbit_topic_map_get_map instead. Returns a small inline preview by default (maxBytes default 2,000, truncated with a flag) plus a permanent blobUrl link to the full content when the artifact is blob-backed — do not raise maxBytes to dump the whole thing into context by default; offer the user the link, or explicitly raise maxBytes only when you genuinely need more inline.

artifactId.description: Artifact id from thorbit_topic_map_get, such as final_output or an artifactPublicId.

maxBytes.description: Max bytes of content to return inline; truncated with a flag if larger. Response always includes blobUrl regardless of this cap — raise maxBytes on a follow-up call, or fetch blobUrl directly, to get the rest.

whenToUse: Use to read one exact research artifact from a known Topic Map run.
avoidWhen: Avoid when the composed completed map is the intended result.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost bounded artifact read with a full-content reference when available.

tools.name: thorbit_topic_map_get
tools.title: Read Topic Map Run Status
tools.description: Poll status, phase, progress, target, model/runtime metadata, and the artifact manifest for a run started by thorbit_topic_map_start. This is the ONLY way to check progress — thorbit_topic_map_start returns immediately, before the run finishes. Poll until status is completed or failed; use detail:"full" instead of the reserved includePhaseData flag when raw phase data is needed.

runPublicId.description: Topic Map run public ID returned by thorbit_topic_map_start.

detail.description: How much run detail to return.

includePhaseData.description: Reserved compatibility flag. Prefer detail=full when raw phase data is needed.

whenToUse: Use to poll an existing Topic Map run until it is completed or failed.
avoidWhen: Avoid before thorbit_topic_map_start returns a real run public ID.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost read of durable Phoenix-projected run state.

tools.name: thorbit_topic_map_get_map
tools.title: Read Topic Map Output
tools.description: Return the finished topic map for a completed run as markdown, json, or presentation-shaped data. Check thorbit_topic_map_get first: this returns not_found (not an error) if the run isn't complete yet. For one specific artifact instead of the composed map, use thorbit_topic_map_artifact_read. For markdown format, returns a small inline preview by default (maxBytes default 2,000, truncated with a flag) plus a permanent blobUrl link to the full content — do not raise maxBytes to dump the whole thing into context by default; offer the user the link, or explicitly raise maxBytes only when you genuinely need more inline. json/presentation formats ignore maxBytes and always return in full.

format.description: Output format for the final map.

maxBytes.description: Max bytes of markdown to return inline (json/presentation formats ignore this); truncated with a flag if larger. Response always includes blobUrl regardless of this cap — raise maxBytes on a follow-up call, or fetch blobUrl directly, to get the rest.

whenToUse: Use to read the composed map after the Topic Map run has completed.
avoidWhen: Avoid while the run is active or when only one research artifact is needed.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost bounded artifact projection with full-content references.

tools.name: thorbit_topic_map_list
tools.title: List Topic Map Runs
tools.description: List past Topic Map runs (most recent first) for a project or the whole org, with status and target. Use this to browse/find a prior run when you already know roughly which project or want recent activity; for a text search across run content and topics, use thorbit_topic_map_search instead.

projectPublicId.description: Optional project filter.

search.description: Optional search text across stored run target metadata.

whenToUse: Use to browse recent Topic Map runs for a project or caller organization.
avoidWhen: Avoid for content search across prior map research; use search instead.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost bounded caller-organization run listing.

tools.name: thorbit_topic_map_search
tools.title: Search Topic Map Runs
tools.description: Full-text search across past Topic Map runs and saved artifacts — matches by topic, competitor, target, or question cluster, not just project name. Use this when looking for prior work by subject rather than browsing recent activity (see thorbit_topic_map_list).

query.description: Text to search across Topic Map run targets and saved artifacts.

projectPublicId.description: Optional project filter.

whenToUse: Use to search prior Topic Map run content by topic, competitor, or cluster.
avoidWhen: Avoid when browsing recent activity without a subject query.
quality.costSummary: Low-cost bounded caller-organization full-text search.

tools.name: thorbit_topic_map_start
tools.title: Start Topic Map Run
tools.description: Start a durable Topic Map run for a Thorbit project — Mastra runtime, OpenRouter MiniMax 3, MCP Scraper web research. Pass targetUrl/domain only if different from the project's own website; brandName, niche, location, icpContent, seedQueries, and competitors all steer the research. Returns a runPublicId plus a thorbit_topic_map_get poll target — the map itself is not returned inline; call thorbit_topic_map_get_map once complete. Metered.

projectPublicId.description: Thorbit project public ID that owns the run.

targetUrl.description: Optional target website URL. If omitted, Thorbit uses the project URL/domain.

domain.description: Optional target domain. Useful when no URL is available.

brandName.description: Optional brand or product name for the target.

niche.description: Optional niche/category context for query planning.

location.description: Optional location context for local or regional topic mapping.

icpContent.description: Optional ICP/customer context to steer the map.

maxCompetitorUrls.description: Maximum competitor URLs to map and classify per site.

serpConcurrency.description: MCP Scraper web concurrency. Hosted Topic Map Lite supports up to 50.

idempotencyKey.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retries.

whenToUse: Use to start a new Mastra-backed topic-map research run for a Thorbit project.
avoidWhen: Avoid when checking an existing run or reading a completed map.
quality.costSummary: Metered hosted Mastra, model, and web-research execution.
sideEffects: Creates and dispatches a durable caller-organization Topic Map run.
May consume Thorbit credits and perform external web research.
